name: Melton, Jeff
evidence: Self-reported as 'owner, Glenview Avenue Holdings' in Austin 78746 (Westlake). 'Jeff Melton' is a common name with 100+ LinkedIn profiles; none corroborated to this zip or entity. Not identified.

name: Felsenthal, David
evidence: David Felsenthal is a Partner at Aleysian, a Chicago-based cloud consulting firm and systems integrator (Salesforce, Salsify, ServiceNow implementation partner); he later became a Managing Director at Spaulding Ridge in Chicago. Self-reported employer 'Aleysian LLC' + occupation 'Partner' + Chicago 60614 (Lincoln Park) match exactly. Distinct from the David Felsenthal who is CEO of EAB in Washington DC. No FEC history (consistent with fec_donation_count 0); the Chicago FEC Felsenthals are Steven A. Felsenthal, a different person.

Mike Jackson; BA in Government, UT Austin.

evidence: No occupation or employer reported ('NA'), no FEC history, and nothing corroborates a specific person. A Holly Hancock is a Los Angeles Superior Court judge elected 8 Nov 2022 (one day before this 9 Nov 2022 gift) and a former LA County deputy public defender, and FEC records show a Seth Hancock, LA County public defender, at this same 90025 zip — but the judge is Holly L. Hancock, not 'Holly Jones Hancock', no spousal or household link is documented, and no connection to Kirk Watson or Austin was found. Not identified; the resemblance is unconfirmed and may be coincidental.

The filing's swapped fields (occupation 'Pearlstone Partners', employer 'Admin') match her title exactly. A UT Austin graduate (Asian Culture and Languages) who has held internal real-estate support roles in marketing and operations since 2017; Austin 78750.

name: Baker, Laura
evidence: Occupation and employer both filed as 'requested' (i.e. the campaign never obtained them). FEC contributions from a Laura Baker in Austin place her at zips 78764 and 78722 (employer Edelman, marketing) — not the 78750 in this record — and total only four small gifts, so the batch's fec_donation_count of 133 appears to be a loose name match rather than this person. 'Laura Baker' is a common name and nothing corroborates a specific individual at 78750. Not identified.

name: Clay, Reed
resolved_employer: Crestline Solutions / Crestline Group — founder and CEO (government affairs / lobbying firm)
evidence: Reed Clay (John R. Clay Jr.) founded and runs Crestline Solutions, an Austin government-affairs and lobbying firm, matching the self-reported 'Consultant, Crestline Solutions' and Austin 78704. He spent eight years with Gov. Greg Abbott, rising from the attorney general's office to Chief Operating Officer for the State of Texas and Abbott's deputy chief of staff. Texas Scorecard ranks him the seventh highest-paid lobbyist in Texas (up to $4,675,000); clients include the Texas Oil and Gas Association, Dow Chemical, Motorola Solutions, Hyundai, NextDecade LNG, Superior HealthPlan, and the Port of Corpus Christi. He is also president of the Texas Nuclear Alliance.

name: Wang, Shin-Tower
resolved_employer: Ensoft, Inc. / Lymon C. Reese & Associates — President and Project Manager
evidence: Shin-Tower Wang, Ph.D., P.E., is president and project manager of Ensoft, Inc./Lymon C. Reese & Associates, an Austin geotechnical-engineering software firm — matching the self-reported 'Project Manager/President, Ensoft' and Austin 78750. A registered professional engineer in Texas and Oklahoma with 40+ years in civil engineering, he holds an M.S. (1982) and Ph.D. (1986) from UT Austin, is a member of its Academy of Distinguished Alumni, and co-authored Wiley's 'Analysis and Design of Shallow and Deep Foundations'. Consults on deep foundations for offshore platforms, wind farms, and long-span bridges.

evidence: James 'Jim' Kozlowski is Senior Managing Director for Private Equity and a named principal at Venturi Wealth Management, an Austin-based independent wealth management firm and multi-family office — matching the self-reported 'Investor, Venturi wealth management' and Austin 78746 (Westlake). He previously co-founded and was president of TGF Management Corp., managing the Texas Growth Fund and Southwest Opportunity Partners, investing more than $700 million across 47 middle-market companies.

name: LaMantia, Greg

evidence: Greg LaMantia is a manager/owner of L&F Distributors, LLC, the McAllen-headquartered Budweiser wholesaler his family has owned and operated since 1977 — matching the self-reported 'Administrator, L&F Dist.
